response:
## Results

We evaluated ten experimental variants of our proposed method on the validation set, each averaged over five random seeds. Our primary comparison is with the baseline configuration (variant_03), which excludes the proposed component and achieves a validation accuracy of 67.8% (±0.6). Among all tested variants, one configuration—variant_08—demonstrates substantial performance gains, achieving a validation accuracy of 70.4% (±0.5), which represents a significant absolute improvement of +2.6% over the baseline.

This result underscores the effectiveness of the proposed modification when appropriately configured. Notably, variant_08 is the only variant that consistently surpasses the baseline across all seeds, suggesting that the benefit is both robust and reliable rather than the result of stochastic variation. The improvement is particularly striking given the strong baseline, and the low standard deviation indicates stable performance.

The remaining variants, which explore alternative parameterizations and ablations, yield validation accuracies ranging from 64.7% to 67.2%, generally trailing the baseline. This highlights the importance of the specific design choices embodied in variant_08; only this configuration fully leverages the capacity of the proposed component.

In summary, our experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method, when instantiated as in variant_08, leads to a clear and reproducible improvement over the best established baseline. These findings validate the core hypothesis of our work: targeted architectural enhancements can deliver meaningful gains on challenging benchmarks.
